# matugen.nvim

### A Material You colorscheme for Neovim that dynamically loads colors generated by matugen

*Author: Senal-D-A-Gunaratna*

## Overview

`matugen.nvim` bridges the gap between Material You color generation and your Neovim environment. Unlike themes that attempt to force compatibility with other editors, `matugen.nvim` uses a dedicated Neovim template system that maps generated colors directly to Neovim highlight groups

This allows you to maintain a perfectly consistent theme across your entire workflow by using the same source colors for both VS Code and Neovim while maintaining native Neovim UI performance and granularity

## Integration

To integrate with `matugen`, use the dedicated template provided in this repository:

1. Copy [nvim-template.jsonc](nvim-template.jsonc) to your `matugen` templates folder
   > **Note:**If you use my [matugen-editors](https://github.com/Senal-D-A-Gunaratna/matugen-editors) setup, you can also use your existing `code-colors.jsonc` if it shares the same structure

2. add this to your matugen `config.toml` :

```toml
[templates.neovim]
input_path = "~/.config/matugen/templates/nvim-template.jsonc"
output_path = "~/.config/matugen/themes/nvim-template.jsonc"
post_hook = "pkill -SIGUSR1 nvim"
```

## Installation

### [lazy.nvim](https://github.com/folke/lazy.nvim)

```lua
return {
  {
    "Senal-D-A-Gunaratna/matugen.nvim",
    lazy = false,
    priority = 1000,
    opts = {
      -- Path to the JSONC file generated by matugen
      jsonc_path = vim.fn.expand("~/.config/matugen/themes/nvim-template.jsonc"),
    },
    config = function(_, opts)
      require("matugen").setup(opts)
      vim.cmd.colorscheme("matugen")
    end,
  },
}
```

## Usage

Set the colorscheme:
```vim
colorscheme matugen
```

Reload the theme manually:
```vim
:lua require('matugen').load()
```

## Signal Support
The plugin listens for `SIGUSR1`. You can trigger a theme reload from your terminal or script:
```bash
pkill -SIGUSR1 nvim
```

## Customization

### Creating Custom Templates
`matugen.nvim` features a modular template system. If you want to extend support for additional plugins or customize specific highlights, see [Creating Custom Templates](doc/TEMPLATES.md)

### Theme Palette
The plugin maps colors to a semantic palette defined in `lua/matugen/palette.lua`. This acts as a centralized API, ensuring that when you add support for a new UI component, your theme remains consistent
